misc.title: In SBS Volume 19, Oda stated that giants mature at half the rate of humans, meaning that a 60-year-old giant would be equivalent to a 30-year-old human. By that logic, a 40-year-old giant would alread
misc.text: In SBS Volume 19, Oda stated that giants mature at half the rate of humans, meaning that a 60-year-old giant would be equivalent to a 30-year-old human. By that logic, a 40-year-old giant would already be considered an adult. However, this is later contradicted in the Elbaph Arc. In Harald's flashback, it is stated that he was equivalent to 15 human years old when he ascended the throne at the age of 45, indicating that giants actually age roughly three times slower than humans. This is also confirmed in the latest SBS (Volume 115), where Oda revealed the ages of the ten kidnapped Elbaph children. Their giant ages are consistently three times their equivalent human ages. (Example: Mag's Giant age = 30, Equivalent Human age = 10) This makes me think about Louis Arnote's warning at the beginning of the Elbaph Arc: "Do not stay in this land for too long." There's a theory that time in Elbaph flows three times slower than in the outside world... but Scopper Gaban seems to have aged normally despite living there. Hmmmm... 🤔☕️ I hope we'll find out the reason behind Louis Arnote's warning by the end of the Arc...
